title: Script-run workstation
description: Configure type = SCRIPT_RUN for script worker dispatch through a SCRIPT_WORKER backend.
openingSummary: SCRIPT_RUN is the WorkstationType for command-backed script worker dispatch. Set type to SCRIPT_RUN when a step must run a SCRIPT_WORKER command and args, and choose behavior independently for STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER scheduling.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with type SCRIPT_RUN and bind a SCRIPT_WORKER. Choose behavior for the scheduling axis you need — STANDARD for fire-once dispatch, REPEATER for change-triggered loops, CRON for schedules, or POLLER for long-lived poller ingress. Wire inputs plus outputs, onContinue, onRejection, and onFailure so script outcomes route correctly. Put command and args on the SCRIPT_WORKER, not on this workstation type. Do not set promptFile, openCodeAgent, classificationRoutes, or operation bindings that belong to other WorkstationType families. Choose AGENT_RUN for agent loops, INFERENCE_RUN for bounded inference, or MODEL_WORKSTATION for prompt-file model steps. SCRIPT_RUN is not a scheduling behavior and is not MODEL_WORKSTATION — keep promptFile and model prompt surfaces on the model-workstation type.

schemaReference.title: Schema reference
schemaReference.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with SCRIPT_RUN overlay applicability. SCRIPT_RUN has no selected exclusive fields. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this type. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a SCRIPT_RUN workstation with behavior STANDARD. The misuse example borrows promptFile from MODEL_WORKSTATION — that field is excluded from SCRIPT_RUN.

links.companionIntro: SCRIPT_RUN requires a SCRIPT_WORKER companion and is compatible with every published WorkstationKind. Bind the script worker, then choose scheduling on the behavior axis.
links.misuseNote: promptFile belongs on MODEL_WORKSTATION. Keep SCRIPT_RUN on identity, worker binding, inputs, outputs, and routing fields only.
